<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:佐伯有清]][[ko:사에키 아리키요]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1925|death_date={{death date|2005|7|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Tokyo|qid=Q1490}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|historian|qid=Q201788}}|awards={{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class|qid=Q56561400}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q13364959]] Wikidata name: Arikiyo Saeki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Image requested|portrait|date=November 2024|cat=People}} {{Infobox academic | name = Arikiyo Saeki | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1925|3|2}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]], [[Akasaka Ward]] | death_date = {{death date and age|2005|7|19|1925|3|2}} | death_place = | othername = | main_interests = [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]] (Ancient Japanese history) | alma_mater = [[:en:Nagoya University|Nagoya University]], [[:en:University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo]] | workplaces = [[:en:Hokkaido University|Hokkaido University]], [[:en:Seijo University|Seijo University]] | degree = [[Doctor of Letters]] | URL = | yearsactive= | spouse = | children = }}
'''Arikiyo Saeki''' (佐伯 有清, March 2, 1925 – July 19, 2005) was a Japanese historian specializing in ancient Japanese history.
== Biography ==
; Early Life and Education Born in 1925 in [[:en:Akasaka|Akasaka]], [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]], Saeki graduated from [[Hosei University Second High School]] and went on to study at the [[Nagoya University Faculty of Letters]] in the Department of Japanese History. He graduated in 1952 and completed a master's degree in Japanese history at the [[University of Tokyo Graduate School]] in 1957.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=佐伯 有清 |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/author/a13491.html |website=吉川弘文館 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
; Academic Career In 1952, Saeki began working at his alma mater, Hosei University Second High School, eventually becoming its principal.<ref name=":0" /> In 1962, he earned a Doctor of Letters from Nagoya University with his thesis on the *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]*. He was appointed professor at [[:en:Hokkaido University|Hokkaido University]] in 1971 and later at [[:en:Seijo University|Seijo University]] in 1983.<ref name=":0" /> He retired from Seijo University in 1995.
Saeki passed away in 2005.
== Awards and Honors == * 1984: Received the 74th [[Japan Academy Prize (Imperial)|Japan Academy Prize]] for *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (Research on the Shinsen Shōjiroku), published by [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]. * Spring 1995: Conferred the [[:en:Order of the Sacred Treasure|Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class.
== Research and Contributions == Saeki’s research primarily focused on three areas: * Ancient [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]] and studies on the [[:en:Yamatai|Yamatai Kingdom]]. * Early Japanese [[:en:Buddhist thought|Buddhist thought]] history. * The formative period of the Japanese [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai sect]].
=== Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan === After his death, his family donated his personal library, leading to the establishment of the Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan in [[Ayase City]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Father's Memory: Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library Established |url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0402/2017/11/17/407611.html |website=タウンニュース |date=2017-11-17 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
== Selected Publications == * *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (9 volumes), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1971–1984. ** On-demand edition, 2007. {{colbegin|2}} # Text Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009157</ref> # Research Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009164</ref> # Commentary Volume 1<ref>ISBN 9784642009171</ref> # Commentary Volume 2<ref>ISBN 9784642009188</ref> # Commentary Volume 3<ref>ISBN 9784642009195</ref> # Commentary Volume 4<ref>ISBN 9784642009201</ref> # Commentary Volume 5<ref>ISBN 9784642009218</ref> # Commentary Volume 6<ref>ISBN 9784642009225</ref> # Index and Essays<ref>ISBN 9784642009232</ref> {{colend}}
* *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū: Shūi-hen*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2001.
=== Other Notable Works === {{colbegin|2}} * *Exploring the Mysteries of Ancient History*, Yomiuri Shimbun, 1973. * *Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1974. * *Postwar Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *Genealogies of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Gakuseisha, 1975. * *The Road to Ancient History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *The Kwanggaeto Stele and the General Staff Headquarters*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1976. * *Profiles of Ancient Japanese History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1977. * *Japanese Ancient Politics and Society*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1978. * *The Last Envoys to Tang China*, Kodansha Gendai Shinsho, 1978. ** Republished as Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 2007. * *Ancient East Asia and Japan*, Kyōiku-sha Rekishi Shinsho, 1979. * *Research History of the Kwanggaeto Stele*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1981. {{colend}}
=== Co-edited Works === * *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1980. * *Basic Anthology on Yamatai-koku* (3 volumes), Sōgensha, 1981–1982. * *Essays on Ancient Japanese Politics*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983.
=== Edited Translations === * *The Records of the Three Kingdoms: Commentary on Japanese References*, Iwanami Bunko, 1988.
== External Links == * [https://sites.google.com/site/seishinkan302 Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan]
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:佐伯有清]][[ko:사에키 아리키요]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1925|death_date={{death date|2005|7|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Tokyo|WD=Q1490}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|historian|WD=Q201788}}|awards={{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class|WD=Q56561400}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13364959]] Wikidata name: Arikiyo Saeki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Image requested|portrait|date=November 2024|cat=People}} {{Infobox academic | name = Arikiyo Saeki | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1925|3|2}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]], [[Akasaka Ward]] | death_date = {{death date and age|2005|7|19|1925|3|2}} | death_place = | othername = | main_interests = [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]] (Ancient Japanese history) | alma_mater = [[:en:Nagoya University|Nagoya University]], [[:en:University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo]] | workplaces = [[:en:Hokkaido University|Hokkaido University]], [[:en:Seijo University|Seijo University]] | degree = [[Doctor of Letters]] | URL = | yearsactive= | spouse = | children = }}
'''Arikiyo Saeki''' (佐伯 有清, March 2, 1925 – July 19, 2005) was a Japanese historian specializing in ancient Japanese history.
== Biography ==
; Early Life and Education Born in 1925 in [[:en:Akasaka|Akasaka]], [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]], Saeki graduated from [[Hosei University Second High School]] and went on to study at the [[Nagoya University Faculty of Letters]] in the Department of Japanese History. He graduated in 1952 and completed a master's degree in Japanese history at the [[University of Tokyo Graduate School]] in 1957.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=佐伯 有清 |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/author/a13491.html |website=吉川弘文館 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
; Academic Career In 1952, Saeki began working at his alma mater, Hosei University Second High School, eventually becoming its principal.<ref name=":0" /> In 1962, he earned a Doctor of Letters from Nagoya University with his thesis on the *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]*. He was appointed professor at [[:en:Hokkaido University|Hokkaido University]] in 1971 and later at [[:en:Seijo University|Seijo University]] in 1983.<ref name=":0" /> He retired from Seijo University in 1995.
Saeki passed away in 2005.
== Awards and Honors == * 1984: Received the 74th [[Japan Academy Prize (Imperial)|Japan Academy Prize]] for *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (Research on the Shinsen Shōjiroku), published by [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]. * Spring 1995: Conferred the [[:en:Order of the Sacred Treasure|Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class.
== Research and Contributions == Saeki’s research primarily focused on three areas: * Ancient [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]] and studies on the [[:en:Yamatai|Yamatai Kingdom]]. * Early Japanese [[:en:Buddhist thought|Buddhist thought]] history. * The formative period of the Japanese [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai sect]].
=== Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan === After his death, his family donated his personal library, leading to the establishment of the Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan in [[Ayase City]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Father's Memory: Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library Established |url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0402/2017/11/17/407611.html |website=タウンニュース |date=2017-11-17 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
== Selected Publications == * *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (9 volumes), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1971–1984. ** On-demand edition, 2007. {{colbegin|2}} # Text Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009157</ref> # Research Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009164</ref> # Commentary Volume 1<ref>ISBN 9784642009171</ref> # Commentary Volume 2<ref>ISBN 9784642009188</ref> # Commentary Volume 3<ref>ISBN 9784642009195</ref> # Commentary Volume 4<ref>ISBN 9784642009201</ref> # Commentary Volume 5<ref>ISBN 9784642009218</ref> # Commentary Volume 6<ref>ISBN 9784642009225</ref> # Index and Essays<ref>ISBN 9784642009232</ref> {{colend}}
* *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū: Shūi-hen*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2001.
=== Other Notable Works === {{colbegin|2}} * *Exploring the Mysteries of Ancient History*, Yomiuri Shimbun, 1973. * *Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1974. * *Postwar Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *Genealogies of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Gakuseisha, 1975. * *The Road to Ancient History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *The Kwanggaeto Stele and the General Staff Headquarters*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1976. * *Profiles of Ancient Japanese History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1977. * *Japanese Ancient Politics and Society*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1978. * *The Last Envoys to Tang China*, Kodansha Gendai Shinsho, 1978. ** Republished as Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 2007. * *Ancient East Asia and Japan*, Kyōiku-sha Rekishi Shinsho, 1979. * *Research History of the Kwanggaeto Stele*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1981. {{colend}}
=== Co-edited Works === * *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1980. * *Basic Anthology on Yamatai-koku* (3 volumes), Sōgensha, 1981–1982. * *Essays on Ancient Japanese Politics*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983.
=== Edited Translations === * *The Records of the Three Kingdoms: Commentary on Japanese References*, Iwanami Bunko, 1988.
== External Links == * [https://sites.google.com/site/seishinkan302 Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan]
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}

'''Arikiyo Saeki''' (佐伯 有清, March 2, 1925 – July 19, 2005) was a Japanese historian specializing in ancient Japanese history.
== Biography ==
; Early Life and Education Born in 1925 in [[:en:Akasaka|Akasaka]], [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]], Saeki graduated from [[Hosei University Second High School]] and went on to study at the [[Nagoya University Faculty of Letters]] in the Department of Japanese History. He graduated in 1952 and completed a master's degree in Japanese history at the [[University of Tokyo Graduate School]] in 1957.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=佐伯 有清 |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/author/a13491.html |website=吉川弘文館 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
; Academic Career In 1952, Saeki began working at his alma mater, Hosei University Second High School, eventually becoming its principal.<ref name=":0" /> In 1962, he earned a Doctor of Letters from Nagoya University with his thesis on the *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]*. He was appointed professor at [[:en:Hokkaido University|Hokkaido University]] in 1971 and later at [[:en:Seijo University|Seijo University]] in 1983.<ref name=":0" /> He retired from Seijo University in 1995.
Saeki passed away in 2005.
== Awards and Honors == * 1984: Received the 74th [[Japan Academy Prize (Imperial)|Japan Academy Prize]] for *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (Research on the Shinsen Shōjiroku), published by [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]. * Spring 1995: Conferred the [[:en:Order of the Sacred Treasure|Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class.
== Research and Contributions == Saeki’s research primarily focused on three areas: * Ancient [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]] and studies on the [[:en:Yamatai|Yamatai Kingdom]]. * Early Japanese [[:en:Buddhist thought|Buddhist thought]] history. * The formative period of the Japanese [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai sect]].
=== Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan === After his death, his family donated his personal library, leading to the establishment of the Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan in [[Ayase City]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Father's Memory: Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library Established |url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0402/2017/11/17/407611.html |website=タウンニュース |date=2017-11-17 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
== Selected Publications == * *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (9 volumes), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1971–1984. ** On-demand edition, 2007. {{colbegin|2}} # Text Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009157</ref> # Research Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009164</ref> # Commentary Volume 1<ref>ISBN 9784642009171</ref> # Commentary Volume 2<ref>ISBN 9784642009188</ref> # Commentary Volume 3<ref>ISBN 9784642009195</ref> # Commentary Volume 4<ref>ISBN 9784642009201</ref> # Commentary Volume 5<ref>ISBN 9784642009218</ref> # Commentary Volume 6<ref>ISBN 9784642009225</ref> # Index and Essays<ref>ISBN 9784642009232</ref> {{colend}}
* *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū: Shūi-hen*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2001.
=== Other Notable Works === {{colbegin|2}} * *Exploring the Mysteries of Ancient History*, Yomiuri Shimbun, 1973. * *Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1974. * *Postwar Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *Genealogies of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Gakuseisha, 1975. * *The Road to Ancient History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *The Kwanggaeto Stele and the General Staff Headquarters*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1976. * *Profiles of Ancient Japanese History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1977. * *Japanese Ancient Politics and Society*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1978. * *The Last Envoys to Tang China*, Kodansha Gendai Shinsho, 1978. ** Republished as Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 2007. * *Ancient East Asia and Japan*, Kyōiku-sha Rekishi Shinsho, 1979. * *Research History of the Kwanggaeto Stele*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1981. {{colend}}
=== Co-edited Works === * *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1980. * *Basic Anthology on Yamatai-koku* (3 volumes), Sōgensha, 1981–1982. * *Essays on Ancient Japanese Politics*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983.
=== Edited Translations === * *The Records of the Three Kingdoms: Commentary on Japanese References*, Iwanami Bunko, 1988.
== External Links == * [https://sites.google.com/site/seishinkan302 Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan]
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}

; Early Life and Education Born in 1925 in [[:en:Akasaka|Akasaka]], [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]], Saeki graduated from [[Hosei University Second High School]] and went on to study at the [[Nagoya University Faculty of Letters]] in the Department of Japanese History. ; Academic Career In 1952, Saeki began working at his alma mater, Hosei University Second High School, eventually becoming its principal.<ref name=":0" /> In 1962, he earned a Doctor of Letters from Nagoya University with his thesis on the *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]*. === Other Notable Works === {{colbegin|2}} * *Exploring the Mysteries of Ancient History*, Yomiuri Shimbun, 1973. * *Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1974. * *Postwar Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *Genealogies of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Gakuseisha, 1975. * *The Road to Ancient History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *The Kwanggaeto Stele and the General Staff Headquarters*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1976. * *Profiles of Ancient Japanese History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1977. * *Japanese Ancient Politics and Society*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1978. * *The Last Envoys to Tang China*, Kodansha Gendai Shinsho, 1978. ** Republished as Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 2007. * *Ancient East Asia and Japan*, Kyōiku-sha Rekishi Shinsho, 1979. * *Research History of the Kwanggaeto Stele*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1981. {{colend}}

'''Arikiyo Saeki''' (佐伯 有清, March 2, 1925 – July 19, 2005) was a Japanese historian specializing in ancient Japanese history.
== Biography ==
; Early Life and Education Born in 1925 in [[:en:Akasaka|Akasaka]], [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]], Saeki graduated from [[Hosei University Second High School]] and went on to study at the [[Nagoya University Faculty of Letters]] in the Department of Japanese History. He graduated in 1952 and completed a master's degree in Japanese history at the [[University of Tokyo Graduate School]] in 1957.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=佐伯 有清 |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/author/a13491.html |website=吉川弘文館 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
; Academic Career In 1952, Saeki began working at his alma mater, Hosei University Second High School, eventually becoming its principal.<ref name=":0" /> In 1962, he earned a Doctor of Letters from Nagoya University with his thesis on the *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]*. He was appointed professor at [[:en:Hokkaido University|Hokkaido University]] in 1971 and later at [[:en:Seijo University|Seijo University]] in 1983.<ref name=":0" /> He retired from Seijo University in 1995.
Saeki passed away in 2005.
== Awards and Honors == * 1984: Received the 74th [[Japan Academy Prize (Imperial)|Japan Academy Prize]] for *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (Research on the Shinsen Shōjiroku), published by [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]. * Spring 1995: Conferred the [[:en:Order of the Sacred Treasure|Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class.
== Research and Contributions == Saeki’s research primarily focused on three areas: * Ancient [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]] and studies on the [[:en:Yamatai|Yamatai Kingdom]]. * Early Japanese [[:en:Buddhist thought|Buddhist thought]] history. * The formative period of the Japanese [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai sect]].
=== Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan === After his death, his family donated his personal library, leading to the establishment of the Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan in [[Ayase City]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Father's Memory: Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library Established |url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0402/2017/11/17/407611.html |website=タウンニュース |date=2017-11-17 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
== Selected Publications == * *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (9 volumes), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1971–1984. ** On-demand edition, 2007. {{colbegin|2}} # Text Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009157</ref> # Research Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009164</ref> # Commentary Volume 1<ref>ISBN 9784642009171</ref> # Commentary Volume 2<ref>ISBN 9784642009188</ref> # Commentary Volume 3<ref>ISBN 9784642009195</ref> # Commentary Volume 4<ref>ISBN 9784642009201</ref> # Commentary Volume 5<ref>ISBN 9784642009218</ref> # Commentary Volume 6<ref>ISBN 9784642009225</ref> # Index and Essays<ref>ISBN 9784642009232</ref> {{colend}}
* *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū: Shūi-hen*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2001.
=== Other Notable Works === {{colbegin|2}} * *Exploring the Mysteries of Ancient History*, Yomiuri Shimbun, 1973. * *Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1974. * *Postwar Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *Genealogies of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Gakuseisha, 1975. * *The Road to Ancient History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *The Kwanggaeto Stele and the General Staff Headquarters*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1976. * *Profiles of Ancient Japanese History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1977. * *Japanese Ancient Politics and Society*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1978. * *The Last Envoys to Tang China*, Kodansha Gendai Shinsho, 1978. ** Republished as Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 2007. * *Ancient East Asia and Japan*, Kyōiku-sha Rekishi Shinsho, 1979. * *Research History of the Kwanggaeto Stele*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1981. {{colend}}
=== Co-edited Works === * *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1980. * *Basic Anthology on Yamatai-koku* (3 volumes), Sōgensha, 1981–1982. * *Essays on Ancient Japanese Politics*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983.
=== Edited Translations === * *The Records of the Three Kingdoms: Commentary on Japanese References*, Iwanami Bunko, 1988.

'''Arikiyo Saeki''' (佐伯 有清, March 2, 1925 – July 19, 2005) was a Japanese historian specializing in ancient Japanese history.
== Biography ==
; Early Life and Education Born in 1925 in [[:en:Akasaka|Akasaka]], [[:en:Tokyo City|Tokyo City]], Saeki graduated from [[Hosei University Second High School]] and went on to study at the [[Nagoya University Faculty of Letters]] in the Department of Japanese History. He graduated in 1952 and completed a master's degree in Japanese history at the [[University of Tokyo Graduate School]] in 1957.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=佐伯 有清 |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/author/a13491.html |website=吉川弘文館 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
; Academic Career In 1952, Saeki began working at his alma mater, Hosei University Second High School, eventually becoming its principal.<ref name=":0" /> In 1962, he earned a Doctor of Letters from Nagoya University with his thesis on the *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]*. He was appointed professor at [[:en:Hokkaido University|Hokkaido University]] in 1971 and later at [[:en:Seijo University|Seijo University]] in 1983.<ref name=":0" /> He retired from Seijo University in 1995.
Saeki passed away in 2005.
== Awards and Honors == * 1984: Received the 74th [[Japan Academy Prize (Imperial)|Japan Academy Prize]] for *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (Research on the Shinsen Shōjiroku), published by [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]. * Spring 1995: Conferred the [[:en:Order of the Sacred Treasure|Order of the Sacred Treasure]], Third Class.
== Research and Contributions == Saeki’s research primarily focused on three areas: * Ancient [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]] and studies on the [[:en:Yamatai|Yamatai Kingdom]]. * Early Japanese [[:en:Buddhist thought|Buddhist thought]] history. * The formative period of the Japanese [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai sect]].
=== Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan === After his death, his family donated his personal library, leading to the establishment of the Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan in [[Ayase City]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Father's Memory: Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library Established |url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0402/2017/11/17/407611.html |website=タウンニュース |date=2017-11-17 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
== Selected Publications == * *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (9 volumes), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1971–1984. ** On-demand edition, 2007. {{colbegin|2}} # Text Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009157</ref> # Research Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009164</ref> # Commentary Volume 1<ref>ISBN 9784642009171</ref> # Commentary Volume 2<ref>ISBN 9784642009188</ref> # Commentary Volume 3<ref>ISBN 9784642009195</ref> # Commentary Volume 4<ref>ISBN 9784642009201</ref> # Commentary Volume 5<ref>ISBN 9784642009218</ref> # Commentary Volume 6<ref>ISBN 9784642009225</ref> # Index and Essays<ref>ISBN 9784642009232</ref> {{colend}}
* *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū: Shūi-hen*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2001.
=== Other Notable Works === {{colbegin|2}} * *Exploring the Mysteries of Ancient History*, Yomiuri Shimbun, 1973. * *Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1974. * *Postwar Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *Genealogies of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Gakuseisha, 1975. * *The Road to Ancient History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *The Kwanggaeto Stele and the General Staff Headquarters*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1976. * *Profiles of Ancient Japanese History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1977. * *Japanese Ancient Politics and Society*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1978. * *The Last Envoys to Tang China*, Kodansha Gendai Shinsho, 1978. ** Republished as Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 2007. * *Ancient East Asia and Japan*, Kyōiku-sha Rekishi Shinsho, 1979. * *Research History of the Kwanggaeto Stele*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1981. {{colend}}
=== Co-edited Works === * *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1980. * *Basic Anthology on Yamatai-koku* (3 volumes), Sōgensha, 1981–1982. * *Essays on Ancient Japanese Politics*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983.
=== Edited Translations === * *The Records of the Three Kingdoms: Commentary on Japanese References*, Iwanami Bunko, 1988.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:佐伯有清]][[ko:사에키 아리키요]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:佐伯有清]][[ko:사에키 아리키요]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:佐伯有清]][[ko:사에키 아리키요]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1925|death_date={{death date|2005|7|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Tokyo|WD=Q1490}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|historian|WD=Q201788}}|awards={{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class|WD=Q56561400}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13364959]] Wikidata name: Arikiyo Saeki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Image requested|portrait|date=November 2024|cat=People}} {{Infobox academic | name = Arikiyo Saeki | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1925|3|2}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} {{ill|Tokyo City|en|Tokyo City|WD=Q1207735|lt=Tokyo City}}, [[Akasaka Ward]] | death_date = {{death date and age|2005|7|19|1925|3|2}} | death_place = | othername = | main_interests = [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]] (Ancient Japanese history) | alma_mater = {{ill|Nagoya University|en|Nagoya University|WD=Q1191132|lt=Nagoya University}}, {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo}} | workplaces = {{ill|Hokkaido University|en|Hokkaido University|WD=Q1057199|lt=Hokkaido University}}, {{ill|Seijo University|en|Seijo University|WD=Q587503|lt=Seijo University}} | degree = [[Doctor of Letters]] | URL = | yearsactive= | spouse = | children = }}
'''Arikiyo Saeki''' (佐伯 有清, March 2, 1925 – July 19, 2005) was a Japanese historian specializing in ancient Japanese history.
== Biography ==
; Early Life and Education Born in 1925 in {{ill|Akasaka|en|Akasaka|WD=Q416122|lt=Akasaka}}, {{ill|Tokyo City|en|Tokyo City|WD=Q1207735|lt=Tokyo City}}, Saeki graduated from [[Hosei University Second High School]] and went on to study at the [[Nagoya University Faculty of Letters]] in the Department of Japanese History. He graduated in 1952 and completed a master's degree in Japanese history at the [[University of Tokyo Graduate School]] in 1957.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=佐伯 有清 |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/author/a13491.html |website=吉川弘文館 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
; Academic Career In 1952, Saeki began working at his alma mater, Hosei University Second High School, eventually becoming its principal.<ref name=":0" /> In 1962, he earned a Doctor of Letters from Nagoya University with his thesis on the *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}*. He was appointed professor at {{ill|Hokkaido University|en|Hokkaido University|WD=Q1057199|lt=Hokkaido University}} in 1971 and later at {{ill|Seijo University|en|Seijo University|WD=Q587503|lt=Seijo University}} in 1983.<ref name=":0" /> He retired from Seijo University in 1995.
Saeki passed away in 2005.
== Awards and Honors == * 1984: Received the 74th [[Japan Academy Prize (Imperial)|Japan Academy Prize]] for *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (Research on the Shinsen Shōjiroku), published by [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]. * Spring 1995: Conferred the {{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure|en|Order of the Sacred Treasure|WD=Q1066052|lt=Order of the Sacred Treasure}}, Third Class.
== Research and Contributions == Saeki’s research primarily focused on three areas: * Ancient [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]] and studies on the {{ill|Yamatai|en|Yamatai|WD=Q844295|lt=Yamatai Kingdom}}. * Early Japanese [[:en:Buddhist thought|Buddhist thought]] history. * The formative period of the Japanese {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai sect}}.
=== Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan === After his death, his family donated his personal library, leading to the establishment of the Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan in [[Ayase City]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Father's Memory: Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library Established |url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0402/2017/11/17/407611.html |website=タウンニュース |date=2017-11-17 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
== Selected Publications == * *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (9 volumes), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1971–1984. ** On-demand edition, 2007. {{colbegin|2}} # Text Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009157</ref> # Research Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009164</ref> # Commentary Volume 1<ref>ISBN 9784642009171</ref> # Commentary Volume 2<ref>ISBN 9784642009188</ref> # Commentary Volume 3<ref>ISBN 9784642009195</ref> # Commentary Volume 4<ref>ISBN 9784642009201</ref> # Commentary Volume 5<ref>ISBN 9784642009218</ref> # Commentary Volume 6<ref>ISBN 9784642009225</ref> # Index and Essays<ref>ISBN 9784642009232</ref> {{colend}}
* *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū: Shūi-hen*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2001.
=== Other Notable Works === {{colbegin|2}} * *Exploring the Mysteries of Ancient History*, Yomiuri Shimbun, 1973. * *Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1974. * *Postwar Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *Genealogies of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Gakuseisha, 1975. * *The Road to Ancient History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *The Kwanggaeto Stele and the General Staff Headquarters*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1976. * *Profiles of Ancient Japanese History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1977. * *Japanese Ancient Politics and Society*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1978. * *The Last Envoys to Tang China*, Kodansha Gendai Shinsho, 1978. ** Republished as Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 2007. * *Ancient East Asia and Japan*, Kyōiku-sha Rekishi Shinsho, 1979. * *Research History of the Kwanggaeto Stele*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1981. {{colend}}
=== Co-edited Works === * *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1980. * *Basic Anthology on Yamatai-koku* (3 volumes), Sōgensha, 1981–1982. * *Essays on Ancient Japanese Politics*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983.
=== Edited Translations === * *The Records of the Three Kingdoms: Commentary on Japanese References*, Iwanami Bunko, 1988.
== External Links == * [https://sites.google.com/site/seishinkan302 Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan]
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:佐伯有清]][[ko:사에키 아리키요]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:佐伯有清]][[ko:사에키 아리키요]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:佐伯有清]][[ko:사에키 아리키요]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1925|death_date={{death date|2005|7|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Tokyo|WD=Q1490}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|historian|WD=Q201788}}|awards={{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class|WD=Q56561400}}}} {{Image requested|portrait|date=November 2024|cat=People}} {{Infobox academic | name = Arikiyo Saeki | image = | image_size = | caption = | birth_date = {{birth date|1925|3|2}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} {{ill|Tokyo City|en|Tokyo City|WD=Q1207735|lt=Tokyo City}}, [[Akasaka Ward]] | death_date = {{death date and age|2005|7|19|1925|3|2}} | death_place = | othername = | main_interests = [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]] (Ancient Japanese history) | alma_mater = {{ill|Nagoya University|en|Nagoya University|WD=Q1191132|lt=Nagoya University}}, {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo}} | workplaces = {{ill|Hokkaido University|en|Hokkaido University|WD=Q1057199|lt=Hokkaido University}}, {{ill|Seijo University|en|Seijo University|WD=Q587503|lt=Seijo University}} | degree = [[Doctor of Letters]] | URL = | yearsactive= | spouse = | children = }}
'''Arikiyo Saeki''' (佐伯 有清, March 2, 1925 – July 19, 2005) was a Japanese historian specializing in ancient Japanese history.
== Biography ==
; Early Life and Education Born in 1925 in {{ill|Akasaka|en|Akasaka|WD=Q416122|lt=Akasaka}}, {{ill|Tokyo City|en|Tokyo City|WD=Q1207735|lt=Tokyo City}}, Saeki graduated from [[Hosei University Second High School]] and went on to study at the [[Nagoya University Faculty of Letters]] in the Department of Japanese History. He graduated in 1952 and completed a master's degree in Japanese history at the [[University of Tokyo Graduate School]] in 1957.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=佐伯 有清 |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/author/a13491.html |website=吉川弘文館 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
; Academic Career In 1952, Saeki began working at his alma mater, Hosei University Second High School, eventually becoming its principal.<ref name=":0" /> In 1962, he earned a Doctor of Letters from Nagoya University with his thesis on the *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}*. He was appointed professor at {{ill|Hokkaido University|en|Hokkaido University|WD=Q1057199|lt=Hokkaido University}} in 1971 and later at {{ill|Seijo University|en|Seijo University|WD=Q587503|lt=Seijo University}} in 1983.<ref name=":0" /> He retired from Seijo University in 1995.
Saeki passed away in 2005.
== Awards and Honors == * 1984: Received the 74th [[Japan Academy Prize (Imperial)|Japan Academy Prize]] for *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (Research on the Shinsen Shōjiroku), published by [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]. * Spring 1995: Conferred the {{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure|en|Order of the Sacred Treasure|WD=Q1066052|lt=Order of the Sacred Treasure}}, Third Class.
== Research and Contributions == Saeki’s research primarily focused on three areas: * Ancient [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]] and studies on the {{ill|Yamatai|en|Yamatai|WD=Q844295|lt=Yamatai Kingdom}}. * Early Japanese [[:en:Buddhist thought|Buddhist thought]] history. * The formative period of the Japanese {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai sect}}.
=== Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan === After his death, his family donated his personal library, leading to the establishment of the Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan in [[Ayase City]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Father's Memory: Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library Established |url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0402/2017/11/17/407611.html |website=タウンニュース |date=2017-11-17 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
== Selected Publications == * *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (9 volumes), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1971–1984. ** On-demand edition, 2007. {{colbegin|2}} # Text Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009157</ref> # Research Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009164</ref> # Commentary Volume 1<ref>ISBN 9784642009171</ref> # Commentary Volume 2<ref>ISBN 9784642009188</ref> # Commentary Volume 3<ref>ISBN 9784642009195</ref> # Commentary Volume 4<ref>ISBN 9784642009201</ref> # Commentary Volume 5<ref>ISBN 9784642009218</ref> # Commentary Volume 6<ref>ISBN 9784642009225</ref> # Index and Essays<ref>ISBN 9784642009232</ref> {{colend}}
* *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū: Shūi-hen*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2001.
=== Other Notable Works === {{colbegin|2}} * *Exploring the Mysteries of Ancient History*, Yomiuri Shimbun, 1973. * *Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1974. * *Postwar Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *Genealogies of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Gakuseisha, 1975. * *The Road to Ancient History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *The Kwanggaeto Stele and the General Staff Headquarters*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1976. * *Profiles of Ancient Japanese History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1977. * *Japanese Ancient Politics and Society*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1978. * *The Last Envoys to Tang China*, Kodansha Gendai Shinsho, 1978. ** Republished as Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 2007. * *Ancient East Asia and Japan*, Kyōiku-sha Rekishi Shinsho, 1979. * *Research History of the Kwanggaeto Stele*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1981. {{colend}}
=== Co-edited Works === * *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1980. * *Basic Anthology on Yamatai-koku* (3 volumes), Sōgensha, 1981–1982. * *Essays on Ancient Japanese Politics*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983.
=== Edited Translations === * *The Records of the Three Kingdoms: Commentary on Japanese References*, Iwanami Bunko, 1988.
== External Links == * [https://sites.google.com/site/seishinkan302 Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan]
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}

'''Arikiyo Saeki''' (佐伯 有清, March 2, 1925 – July 19, 2005) was a Japanese historian specializing in ancient Japanese history.
== Biography ==
; Early Life and Education Born in 1925 in {{ill|Akasaka|en|Akasaka|WD=Q416122|lt=Akasaka|1=Akasaka}}, {{ill|Tokyo City|en|Tokyo City|WD=Q1207735|lt=Tokyo City|1=Tokyo City}}, Saeki graduated from [[Hosei University Second High School]] and went on to study at the [[Nagoya University Faculty of Letters]] in the Department of Japanese History. He graduated in 1952 and completed a master's degree in Japanese history at the [[University of Tokyo Graduate School]] in 1957.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=佐伯 有清 |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/author/a13491.html |website=吉川弘文館 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
; Academic Career In 1952, Saeki began working at his alma mater, Hosei University Second High School, eventually becoming its principal.<ref name=":0" /> In 1962, he earned a Doctor of Letters from Nagoya University with his thesis on the *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}*. He was appointed professor at {{ill|Hokkaido University|en|Hokkaido University|WD=Q1057199|lt=Hokkaido University|1=Hokkaido University}} in 1971 and later at {{ill|Seijo University|en|Seijo University|WD=Q587503|lt=Seijo University|1=Seijo University}} in 1983.<ref name=":0" /> He retired from Seijo University in 1995.
Saeki passed away in 2005.
== Awards and Honors == * 1984: Received the 74th [[Japan Academy Prize (Imperial)|Japan Academy Prize]] for *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (Research on the Shinsen Shōjiroku), published by [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]. * Spring 1995: Conferred the {{ill|Order of the Sacred Treasure|en|Order of the Sacred Treasure|WD=Q1066052|lt=Order of the Sacred Treasure|1=Order of the Sacred Treasure}}, Third Class.
== Research and Contributions == Saeki’s research primarily focused on three areas: * Ancient [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]] and studies on the {{ill|Yamatai|en|Yamatai|WD=Q844295|lt=Yamatai Kingdom|1=Yamatai}}. * Early Japanese [[:en:Buddhist thought|Buddhist thought]] history. * The formative period of the Japanese {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai sect|1=Tiantai}}.
=== Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan === After his death, his family donated his personal library, leading to the establishment of the Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan in [[Ayase City]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Father's Memory: Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library Established |url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0402/2017/11/17/407611.html |website=タウンニュース |date=2017-11-17 |access-date=2022-07-09}}</ref>
== Selected Publications == * *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū* (9 volumes), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1971–1984. ** On-demand edition, 2007. {{colbegin|2}} # Text Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009157</ref> # Research Volume<ref>ISBN 9784642009164</ref> # Commentary Volume 1<ref>ISBN 9784642009171</ref> # Commentary Volume 2<ref>ISBN 9784642009188</ref> # Commentary Volume 3<ref>ISBN 9784642009195</ref> # Commentary Volume 4<ref>ISBN 9784642009201</ref> # Commentary Volume 5<ref>ISBN 9784642009218</ref> # Commentary Volume 6<ref>ISBN 9784642009225</ref> # Index and Essays<ref>ISBN 9784642009232</ref> {{colend}}
* *Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū: Shūi-hen*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2001.
=== Other Notable Works === {{colbegin|2}} * *Exploring the Mysteries of Ancient History*, Yomiuri Shimbun, 1973. * *Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1974. * *Postwar Yamatai Research History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *Genealogies of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Gakuseisha, 1975. * *The Road to Ancient History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1975. * *The Kwanggaeto Stele and the General Staff Headquarters*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1976. * *Profiles of Ancient Japanese History*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1977. * *Japanese Ancient Politics and Society*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1978. * *The Last Envoys to Tang China*, Kodansha Gendai Shinsho, 1978. ** Republished as Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 2007. * *Ancient East Asia and Japan*, Kyōiku-sha Rekishi Shinsho, 1979. * *Research History of the Kwanggaeto Stele*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1981. {{colend}}
=== Co-edited Works === * *Japanese Ancient History Essays*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1980. * *Basic Anthology on Yamatai-koku* (3 volumes), Sōgensha, 1981–1982. * *Essays on Ancient Japanese Politics*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983.
=== Edited Translations === * *The Records of the Three Kingdoms: Commentary on Japanese References*, Iwanami Bunko, 1988.
== External Links == * [https://sites.google.com/site/seishinkan302 Saeki Arikiyo Memorial Library: Seishinkan]
